features.summary: Procter & Gamble delivered strong Q3 results with 4% organic sales growth and 8% core EPS growth, maintaining raised full-year guidance. The company achieved 40 basis points of global market share gains while managing significant supply chain challenges including commodity cost increases, FX headwinds, and COVID-related disruptions. E-commerce sales grew 50% year-to-date, now representing 14% of global sales. P&G announced a 10% dividend increase and expects to return approximately $19 billion to shareholders this fiscal year.
features.management_outlook: Management maintains organic sales growth guidance of 5-6% and core EPS growth of 8-10% for fiscal year, while raising free cash flow productivity targets above 100%. The company expects continued volatility in Q4 with challenging comparisons in the U.S. and China, plus ongoing soft conditions in Europe and enterprise markets. For fiscal 2022, management anticipates larger commodity headwinds but plans to offset through pricing actions and productivity improvements, with initial outlook to be provided in July.
features.risks: Significant commodity cost increases present the largest near-term risk, with $125 million after-tax headwind now forecast versus initial guidance. Transportation costs have risen to $200 million after-tax impact due to driver shortages and elevated freight rates. Foreign exchange headwinds total $150 million after-tax. COVID-related disruptions continue in key markets like India and Brazil, with potential for supply chain interruptions, channel closures, and economic distress affecting category growth.
features.key_drivers: Strong innovation pipeline continues driving market share gains, with Dawn Powerwash contributing $150 million in first-year sales and Pampers Baby Dry launching improved formulation. Pricing actions announced for Baby Care, Feminine Care, and adult incontinence in the U.S. effective mid-September, with mid-to-high single-digit increases. Productivity programs remain embedded in company culture to help offset cost pressures. E-commerce growth and premiumization strategies support mix improvements despite some margin dilution from faster-growing lower-margin categories.
